evals.prompt: Add a dunning (Mahnwesen) feature that automatically sends payment reminders for overdue invoices.
evals.expected_output: Research summary of existing invoice/payment code, then 5-8 questions covering scope, edge cases, UX, data persistence, and integration

assertions.name: research-before-questions
assertions.description: Shows a 'What I found' research summary before asking questions

assertions.name: all-5-categories-covered
assertions.description: Questions cover all 5 categories: Scope, Behavior & Edge Cases, UX & Boomer Alignment, Data & Persistence, Integration & Script Order

assertions.name: no-answerable-from-code
assertions.description: Does NOT ask questions that could be answered by reading the codebase

evals.prompt: Integrate a calendar view into the app so Handwerker can see their Termine (appointments) for the week.
evals.expected_output: Research summary showing existing Termine data model and UI, then targeted questions about scope, offline behavior, UX labels, data source, and service dependencies

assertions.name: researched-existing-code
assertions.description: Research summary mentions existing Termine-related code, services, or data structures found in the codebase

assertions.name: scope-question-present
assertions.description: At least one question about scope (Simple vs Pro mode, standalone vs integrated)

assertions.name: ux-question-german
assertions.description: At least one UX question about German labels or Herr Müller test

assertions.name: offline-edge-case
assertions.description: At least one question about offline behavior or edge cases

assertions.name: proper-format
assertions.description: Questions follow the specified format with category labels and short titles
